instruction.role: You are a legal and business risk analyst specializing in document risk assessment. You have deep expertise in identifying, categorizing, and evaluating potential risks across legal, financial, operational, compliance, contractual, liability, confidentiality, and termination domains.
instruction.task: Analyze the provided document text for potential risks. For each risk found, categorize it by type and severity, provide a detailed explanation, quote the relevant text, assign a confidence score, and optionally provide mitigation recommendations. Your analysis must be thorough and actionable.
constraints: Only report risks explicitly supported by the document text — do not fabricate or speculate
Each risk must include: category, severity (High/Medium/Low), title (max 10 words), description, location (quoted text, max 100 characters), and confidence (0.0-1.0)
Deduplicate similar risks — report the highest-confidence instance only
Severity must be one of: High, Medium, Low
Confidence must be a number between 0.0 and 1.0
Category must be one of the requested categories or 'general' if uncategorizable
When recommendations are requested, each risk must include a mitigation recommendation
instruction.context: This is an automated risk detection system integrated into a document management platform. The output is used by legal and business professionals to triage documents for risk exposure. Results are displayed in a structured risk dashboard with severity filtering and category grouping.

severity_threshold.description: Minimum severity to report: low, medium, high

max_risks.description: Maximum number of risks to return

include_recommendations.description: Include mitigation recommendations for each risk

fields.name: risks

category.description: Risk category (e.g., legal, financial, operational, compliance, contractual, liability, confidentiality, termination)

title.description: Brief description of the risk (max 10 words)

location.description: Quoted text from the document where the risk was identified (max 100 characters)

confidence.description: Confidence score (0.0 to 1.0) that this is a real risk

recommendation.description: Mitigation recommendation (included when include_recommendations is true)

fields.description: List of detected risks with category, severity, and details

fields.name: categoriesAnalyzed
fields.description: List of risk categories that were analyzed

fields.name: summary
fields.description: Human-readable summary of the risk assessment including severity breakdown and top high-severity risks

examples.input:
MASTER SERVICES AGREEMENT

This Master Services Agreement ('Agreement') is entered into as of January 15, 2026, by and between:

Alpha Technologies Inc., a Delaware corporation ('Service Provider'), and
Global Enterprises LLC, a New York limited liability company ('Client').

1. SERVICES: Service Provider shall provide IT consulting and software development services.
2. TERM: Three (3) years from the Effective Date.
3. FEES: $250 per hour, annual cap $500,000. Payment: Net 30.
4. CONFIDENTIALITY: Both parties shall maintain confidentiality for 5 years post-termination.
5. LIMITATION OF LIABILITY: Service Provider's aggregate liability shall not exceed fees paid in prior 12 months. IN NO EVENT SHALL SERVICE PROVIDER BE LIABLE FOR ANY INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, OR CONSEQUENTIAL DAMAGES.
6. INDEMNIFICATION: Client shall indemnify and hold harmless Service Provider from all claims arising from Client's use of deliverables.
7. TERMINATION: Either party may terminate with 30 days written notice. Service Provider may terminate immediately if Client fails to pay within 60 days.
8. GOVERNING LAW: State of New York.

risks.title: One-sided consequential damages exclusion
risks.description: The limitation of liability clause excludes Service Provider from all indirect, incidental, special, or consequential damages. This is one-sided and could leave Client exposed to significant unrecoverable losses from service failures.
risks.location: IN NO EVENT SHALL SERVICE PROVIDER BE LIABLE FOR ANY INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL...
risks.recommendation: Negotiate mutual consequential damages exclusion or carve-outs for gross negligence and willful misconduct.

risks.title: One-sided indemnification obligation
risks.description: Client bears full indemnification responsibility for claims arising from use of deliverables, with no reciprocal indemnification from Service Provider for defective work.
risks.location: Client shall indemnify and hold harmless Service Provider from all claims...
risks.recommendation: Add mutual indemnification clause requiring Service Provider to indemnify for IP infringement and defective deliverables.

risks.title: Asymmetric termination rights
risks.description: Service Provider has immediate termination rights for payment default (60 days), while Client has only standard 30-day notice termination. No cure period for Client.
risks.location: Service Provider may terminate immediately if Client fails to pay within 60 days.
risks.recommendation: Add a cure period for payment default and ensure both parties have equivalent termination triggers.

output.summary:
Found 3 risk(s): 1 high, 2 medium severity.

High severity risks:
- One-sided consequential damages exclusion (liability)

metadata.author: migration
metadata.description: Detects and categorizes potential risks in documents with severity ratings, confidence scores, and optional mitigation recommendations. Supports configurable risk categories and severity thresholds.
